meaning: To try new things or expand into new areas
example: After 10 years in accounting, she decided to branch out into consulting.

meaning: To rescue someone from a difficult situation; to abandon a plan
example: His parents bailed him out when he missed the rent.

meaning: To be patient with someone or something
example: Bear with me while I pull up the file.

meaning: To be summarized or reduced to the most important point
example: It all boils down to trust and communication.

meaning: To move something to an earlier date; to propose something
example: The meeting has been brought forward to Monday.

meaning: To give in to pressure; to collapse
example: Management caved in after employees threatened to strike.

meaning: To resolve a misunderstanding; for weather to become clear
example: Let me clear up any confusion about the deadline.

meaning: To volunteer information or to step up
example: No witnesses came forward after the incident.

meaning: To succeed; to give a certain impression
example: His apology didn't come off as sincere.

meaning: To interrupt a conversation or to move in front of someone
example: Sorry to cut in, but I need to add something.

meaning: To last longer than expected in a boring way
example: The meeting dragged on for three hours.

meaning: To become less intense; to relax
example: The pressure at work finally eased up after the deadline.

meaning: To resist or keep something away
example: She fended off the criticism by staying calm.

meaning: To make something more definite or solid
example: Let's firm up the details before we announce anything.

meaning: To pursue something further after an initial action
example: I'll follow up with the client after the presentation.

meaning: To prepare for something
example: The whole team is gearing up for the product launch.

meaning: To reach an agreement after long discussion
example: They finally hammered out a deal after weeks of negotiations.

meaning: To delay doing something; to keep something at bay
example: Let's hold off on making any decisions until we have more data.

meaning: To enter a conversation or activity enthusiastically
example: Feel free to jump in if you have any questions.

meaning: To explain something clearly; to arrange items
example: Let me lay out the plan step by step.

meaning: To stop increasing or decreasing and stay steady
example: After months of growth, sales finally leveled off.

meaning: To finalize the details of something
example: We need to nail down the budget before moving forward.

meaning: To develop in a successful way
example: I hope this new strategy pans out.

meaning: To contribute help or money
example: If everyone pitches in, we'll finish by noon.

meaning: To develop or unfold over time
example: Let's see how this situation plays out.

meaning: To succeed in doing something difficult
example: She pulled off an amazing presentation with no preparation.

meaning: To reschedule to a later time; to resist or oppose
example: The launch was pushed back by two weeks.

meaning: To increase in speed, intensity, or production
example: We need to ramp up production to meet demand.

meaning: To launch or introduce something gradually
example: The new software will be rolled out next quarter.

meaning: To eliminate something as a possibility
example: We can't rule out the possibility of delays.

meaning: To quickly mention or check something with someone
example: Let me run this idea by my manager first.

meaning: To reduce the size or scope of something
example: We had to scale back our plans due to budget cuts.

meaning: To recognize the truth behind a deception; to complete something
example: I can see through his excuses — he just doesn't want to come.

meaning: To make major changes to something
example: The new CEO shook up the entire company structure.

meaning: To be fully understood or realized
example: The news hasn't fully sunk in yet.

meaning: To make a careless mistake
example: I slipped up and called him by the wrong name.

meaning: To settle or put in order
example: Let me get this squared away before the weekend.

meaning: To take on more responsibility; to increase
example: Someone needs to step up and lead this project.

meaning: To extend over a long time; to lie down and relax
example: The project stretched out over several months.

meaning: To make something stricter or more secure
example: We need to tighten up our security procedures.

meaning: To briefly mention or deal with a topic
example: She touched on several key issues in her speech.

meaning: To deceive someone into doing something
example: He was tricked into signing the contract.

meaning: To stop paying attention to something
example: After an hour of the lecture, I started tuning out.

meaning: To gradually disappear or diminish
example: The excitement of a new job wears off after a while.

meaning: To give your opinion on something
example: Would you like to weigh in on this decision?

meaning: To gradually slow down or come to an end
example: The party started winding down around midnight.

meaning: To develop or build gradually; to make yourself feel something
example: I need to work up the courage to ask for a raise.

meaning: To focus closely on something
example: Let's zero in on the main problem here.

meaning: To stop paying attention and daydream
example: Sorry, I zoned out — what were you saying?

meaning: To focus on a specific detail more closely
example: The analysis zooms in on customer behavior in Q3.

meaning: To work late into the night
example: I've been burning the midnight oil to finish this report.

meaning: Something has started and is in progress
example: Now that funding is secured, the ball is rolling.

meaning: To understand a hidden meaning
example: Reading between the lines, I think she wants more recognition.

meaning: To do something that harms your own interests
example: He shot himself in the foot by arguing with the client.

word: get a taste of your own medicine
meaning: To experience what you make others go through
example: He was always interrupting people — now he's getting a taste of his own medicine.

meaning: An obvious problem that no one wants to discuss
korean: 모두가 알지만 말 못 하는 문제
example: His drinking was the elephant in the room at every family dinner.

meaning: To say or do something unfair or cruel
example: Bringing up his past mistakes in public was hitting below the belt.

meaning: To change the rules or expectations unfairly
example: Every time we meet the targets, they move the goalposts.

word: the writing is on the wall
meaning: The signs of something bad are clear
example: The writing is on the wall — they're going to close the department.

meaning: To fail or die
example: Three of our competitors bit the dust during the recession.

meaning: To raise a false alarm repeatedly
example: He cried wolf so many times that no one believed him.

meaning: To become nervous or hesitant before something
example: She got cold feet right before the wedding.

word: have a chip on your shoulder
meaning: To feel resentful because of past unfair treatment
example: He's had a chip on his shoulder ever since he was passed over for promotion.

meaning: In serious trouble
example: He's in hot water after missing the deadline again.

meaning: To avoid drawing attention to yourself
example: After the scandal, she decided to keep a low profile.

meaning: Something you don't enjoy or prefer
example: Reality TV is really not my cup of tea.

meaning: To create a complicated situation by addressing something
example: Bringing that up will open a can of worms.

meaning: To shift responsibility to someone else
example: He's always passing the buck instead of owning up.

meaning: To do something that upsets the current situation
example: He didn't want to rock the boat by complaining.

meaning: To agree with someone
example: We don't always see eye to eye, but we respect each other.

meaning: To sell very quickly in large quantities
example: The new phone is selling like hotcakes.

meaning: To maintain your position or opinion under pressure
example: She stood her ground despite the criticism.

meaning: To cause problems or conflict
example: He always stirs up trouble wherever he goes.

meaning: To hide a problem instead of dealing with it
example: You can't keep sweeping these issues under the rug.

meaning: The final problem that makes a situation unbearable
korean: 마지막 한 방울, 참을 수 없게 만드는 마지막 것
example: Forgetting our anniversary was the last straw.

meaning: To approach a problem in a creative way
example: We need to think outside the box to solve this.

meaning: To deliberately ignore something wrong
example: Management turned a blind eye to the bullying.

meaning: Experiencing stress or urgency to perform
example: She performs best when under pressure.

meaning: To have many different roles or responsibilities
example: In a small company, you wear many hats.

meaning: Something that will never happen
example: He'll clean his room when pigs fly.

meaning: To start something; to initiate action
example: Let's get the ball rolling on this new initiative.

meaning: Very common and not valuable
example: English tutors are a dime a dozen online.

meaning: To get to the important point without delay
example: Let's cut to the chase — are you interested or not?

word: go back to the drawing board
meaning: To start planning something again from the beginning
example: The proposal was rejected, so we're going back to the drawing board.

meaning: To be in a position of advantage
example: Knowing multiple languages gives you the upper hand in interviews.

word: in the blink of an eye
meaning: Very quickly
example: Twenty years passed in the blink of an eye.

word: make a mountain out of a molehill
meaning: To exaggerate a small problem
example: Don't make a mountain out of a molehill — it's just a small delay.

meaning: To say something directly
example: I won't beat around the bush — the results were disappointing.

meaning: In agreement; sharing the same understanding
example: Before we start, let's make sure we're all on the same page.

meaning: To use personal influence to get something done
example: He pulled strings to get his son into the company.

meaning: To manage something in a strict and efficient way
example: Our manager runs a tight ship — everyone is always on time.

word: the ball is in their court
meaning: It's their turn to decide or take action
example: I've sent the offer. The ball is in their court now.

meaning: In all circumstances, good or bad
example: She's been there for me through thick and thin.

meaning: Under pressure to meet a deadline
example: We're under the gun to finish this by Friday.

meaning: To be very careful not to upset someone
example: I feel like I'm always walking on eggshells around him.

word: burn the candle at both ends
meaning: To work too hard with too little rest
example: She's been burning the candle at both ends and is exhausted.

meaning: To organize yourself and behave more responsibly
example: You really need to get your act together before the deadline.

meaning: To reach a point where progress stops
example: I hit a wall after two hours of studying and couldn't focus.

meaning: To wait for a situation to calm down before acting
example: Let the dust settle before you respond to that email.

word: take it with a grain of salt
meaning: To not believe something completely
example: Take his advice with a grain of salt — he's not an expert.

word: I wanted to follow up on my previous message
meaning: Checking in about an email or request that hasn't been answered
korean: 이전 메시지에 대해 다시 확인하고 싶었습니다
example: I wanted to follow up on my previous message regarding the proposal.

word: I hope I'm not catching you at a bad time
meaning: A polite way to check if the timing is convenient
example: I hope I'm not catching you at a bad time — I have a quick question.

word: Thank you for taking the time to meet with me
meaning: Expressing gratitude after a meeting
example: Thank you for taking the time to meet with me yesterday.

word: I wanted to bring this to your attention
meaning: Formally alerting someone to something important
example: I wanted to bring this to your attention before the end of day.

word: Please feel free to reach out
meaning: Inviting someone to contact you without hesitation
example: Please feel free to reach out if you have any concerns.

meaning: Saying you are willing to help
example: I'm happy to assist with any questions you may have.

meaning: Verifying information previously discussed
example: Just to confirm, the meeting is at 2 PM on Thursday.

meaning: Assuming the recipient already knows this information
example: As you may be aware, our policy changed last month.

word: I'd like to take this opportunity to
meaning: Using this occasion to do something formally
example: I'd like to take this opportunity to thank the entire team.

meaning: Making sure that something happens
example: Please ensure that all forms are submitted by Friday.

word: This is to inform you that
meaning: A formal way to start a notification
example: This is to inform you that your account has been updated.

word: We would like to invite you to
meaning: Formally inviting someone to participate
example: We would like to invite you to our annual conference.

word: I trust you had a great weekend
meaning: A friendly opener on Monday emails
example: I trust you had a great weekend. I wanted to share an update.

word: Could we arrange a call to discuss this further
meaning: Suggesting a phone call or video meeting
korean: 이에 대해 더 논의하기 위해 통화를 잡을 수 있을까요
example: Could we arrange a call to discuss this further at your convenience?

word: I'm writing to express my concern about
meaning: Formally raising a concern or complaint
example: I'm writing to express my concern about the recent billing error.

meaning: Having thought about something thoroughly before responding
example: After careful consideration, we have decided to decline the offer.

meaning: Thanking someone for their ongoing help or loyalty
example: We appreciate your continued support of our organization.

word: I look forward to working with you
meaning: Expressing enthusiasm for future collaboration
example: I look forward to working with you on this project.

word: We apologize for any confusion this may have caused
meaning: Formally apologizing for miscommunication
example: We apologize for any confusion this may have caused and will clarify shortly.

word: Please do not reply to this email
meaning: An automated message instruction
example: Please do not reply to this email. Contact support at help@company.com.

word: I wanted to check in and see how things are progressing
meaning: A friendly check on the status of something
example: I wanted to check in and see how things are progressing on your end.

meaning: Formally sharing good news with a group
example: We are pleased to announce the launch of our new product line.

meaning: Having done something without being asked, hoping it's okay
example: I've taken the liberty of scheduling a meeting for next Tuesday.

meaning: Asking someone to ignore an email you sent by mistake
example: Please disregard my previous email — I sent it to the wrong person.

word: I will be out of the office from
meaning: Notifying absence from the office
example: I will be out of the office from July 4th to July 8th.

meaning: Providing information for someone to look at
example: For your reference, I've attached last year's report.

meaning: A very polite way to make a request
example: Would it be possible to extend the deadline by one week?

meaning: Including someone else on the email thread
example: I'm copying in our finance team who can help with this query.

word: This falls outside my area of expertise
meaning: Saying something is not your field or responsibility
example: This falls outside my area of expertise — I'll refer you to our legal team.

meaning: Considering the information just mentioned
example: In light of the above, I recommend we reconsider the timeline.

word: I'll have this to you by
meaning: Committing to deliver something by a specific time
example: I'll have this to you by end of business today.

meaning: Thanking someone for including you in the conversation
example: Thanks for looping me in — I'll take it from here.

meaning: Trusting someone else to make the final call
example: I'll defer to your judgment on this one since you're the expert.

word: We'd love to get your thoughts on
meaning: Asking for someone's opinion in a friendly way
example: We'd love to get your thoughts on the attached proposal.

word: As a follow-up to our meeting
meaning: Referencing a meeting that just took place
example: As a follow-up to our meeting, I've outlined the action items below.

meaning: Gently reminding someone of something
example: Please be reminded that the registration deadline is tomorrow.

word: I'm available anytime that works for you
meaning: Being flexible about scheduling
example: I'm available anytime that works for you — just let me know.

word: Please advise on the best way forward
meaning: Asking someone how to proceed
example: I'm unsure how to handle this — please advise on the best way forward.

word: I'd be happy to jump on a call
meaning: Offering to have a phone or video call
korean: 전화나 화상 통화로 얘기 나누면 좋겠습니다
example: If it's easier, I'd be happy to jump on a call to discuss.

meaning: Thanking someone before they do something
example: Please review the attached document. Thanks in advance!

meaning: A friendly professional email sign-off
example: Warm regards, Sunny Park

meaning: Closing with a hope that the information was useful
example: I've included some resources at the bottom. Hope this helps!

meaning: Politely drawing attention to something important
example: Kindly note that our office will be relocating next month.

word: I'm reaching out on behalf of
meaning: Contacting someone as a representative
example: I'm reaching out on behalf of our marketing director.

word: Please let me know your availability
meaning: Asking when someone is free to meet
example: Please let me know your availability for a 30-minute call this week.

meaning: Expressing appreciation for a business relationship
example: We value your partnership and look forward to continued collaboration.

meaning: Appreciating someone's patience or flexibility
example: The project will be delayed by one week. Thank you for your understanding.

meaning: Giving a brief recap of what was talked about
example: To summarize our discussion, we agreed on three key action items.

meaning: Directing someone to information included in the email body
example: Please find below the details of the upcoming event.

meaning: Expressing certainty about a positive outcome
example: I'm confident that we can resolve this issue by the end of the week.

word: What are you up to these days?
meaning: Asking what someone has been doing lately
example: Long time no see! What are you up to these days?

word: We should do this again sometime
meaning: Suggesting to repeat a pleasant activity
example: I had such a good time today — we should do this again sometime.

word: I've been meaning to call you
meaning: I intended to contact you but kept forgetting
example: I've been meaning to call you — how have things been?

meaning: A long time has passed since we last spoke or met
example: It's been a while — we need to catch up properly!

meaning: Asking why something happened or what caused it
korean: 왜 그런 거야? 무슨 일이 있었어?
example: You seem upset — what brought that on?

meaning: Please don't misunderstand what I'm saying
example: Don't get me wrong — I love your cooking, I'm just full.

meaning: In my opinion
example: If you ask me, they should have planned this better.

meaning: Acknowledging that someone made a valid argument
example: You've got a point — maybe we should wait before deciding.

meaning: However; but at the same time
example: The commute is terrible. That said, the job itself is great.

meaning: Regardless of which option is chosen
example: Either way, we should make a decision soon.

meaning: I have absolutely no interest or concern
example: I couldn't care less what he thinks about me.

word: It's not the end of the world
meaning: It's not as serious as it seems
example: You didn't get the part — it's not the end of the world.

meaning: Anything is possible; don't assume
example: Should I apply? — You never know, you might get it!

meaning: That's just how life works; accept it
example: I didn't get the promotion. — That's the way it goes sometimes.

meaning: I'm ready and eager to listen
example: You said you had big news? I'm all ears!

meaning: What's the hidden problem or condition?
example: Free tickets? What's the catch?

meaning: It's so obvious it doesn't need to be stated
example: It goes without saying that honesty is important.

word: I'm on the fence about it
meaning: I haven't made up my mind yet
example: Are you going to the reunion? — I'm on the fence about it.

meaning: Do what you want; I don't mind
example: I'm not going — suit yourself, but you'll miss out.

meaning: Just asking because I'm curious, for no particular reason
example: Out of curiosity, how did you two meet?

meaning: I agree with your position
example: The pricing is too high. — I'm with you on that.

meaning: Expressing disbelief, encouragement, or urging
example: Come on, you can do it — just try!

meaning: What you said is an understatement
example: The presentation was a disaster. — That's putting it mildly.

meaning: Your comment made me think of something I hadn't considered
example: Now that you mention it, she did seem a bit quiet at dinner.

meaning: I don't think so, but I'm not certain
example: Has the schedule changed? — Not that I know of.

meaning: What's going on with someone or something?
example: What's the story with the new guy in accounting?

meaning: There's no rush; proceed at your own pace
example: Need a moment to think? Take your time.

meaning: I don't believe what you're saying
example: He said he was stuck in traffic for two hours — I'm not buying it.

meaning: Everyone has different preferences and that's okay
example: I don't like spicy food, but to each their own.

word: At the end of the day
meaning: When everything is considered; ultimately
example: At the end of the day, what matters is that you're happy.

meaning: The answer might be unexpected
example: Is Korean food popular here? — You'd be surprised, it really is.

meaning: That's irrelevant to the main topic
example: Yes, but he was late — that's beside the point right now.

meaning: Try it; attempt it
example: You've never tried surfing? You should give it a shot!

meaning: I completely agree with your statement
example: The prices here are insane! — You're telling me.

meaning: I'm not naive; I know what's going on
example: I wasn't born yesterday — I can see exactly what he's doing.

meaning: Be less critical of me; give me a break
example: I just started this job — cut me some slack!

meaning: I'll decline; no thanks
example: Want some more dessert? — I'll pass, I'm stuffed.

meaning: Let's stop working for today
example: We've been at this for six hours — let's call it a day.

meaning: The past cannot be changed, so move on
example: I made a mistake, but what's done is done — I'll move forward.

meaning: Don't worry about it
example: Sorry I forgot to text back! — Don't sweat it, I wasn't worried.

meaning: Does that plan or idea work for you?
example: We could meet at 6 for dinner — how does that sound?

meaning: I'm willing; I want to participate
example: Road trip this weekend? — I'm game!

meaning: Include me; I want to join
example: We're going bowling Saturday night — count me in!

meaning: That sounds familiar to me
example: Do you know someone named Mark Lee? — That name rings a bell.

meaning: I can't remember or think of something right now
example: What was the name of that restaurant? — I'm drawing a blank.

meaning: I forgot about it
example: Sorry I didn't call — it completely slipped my mind.

word: It's on the tip of my tongue
meaning: I almost remember but can't quite recall
example: What's that actor's name? It's on the tip of my tongue.

meaning: I understand your reasoning
example: I see your point, but I still think we should wait.

meaning: I accept that I was wrong
example: Actually it opened in 2015, not 2016. — I stand corrected.

meaning: I completely agree with what you just said
example: This summer heat is unbearable! — You can say that again.
